name: Mom's Baklava
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Walnuts or pecans, ground (1 lb)
recipeInstructions:
1. Spread nuts in a shallow baking pan and toast at 300 degrees F. for 10 minutes or until lightly browned. Cool.
2. Combine 3/4 cup sugar, water, and cinnamon stick in a saucepan. Boil until clear. Add honey and heat until well-blended. Remove from heat, let cool, and remove cinnamon stick.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1/2 cup sugar, lemon peel, and ground cinnamon. Mash together with the back of a spoon to blend the citrus oil with sugar.
4. Add the cooled toasted nuts and mix well.
5. Butter a 9x13 inch pan. Line it with 3 sheets of filo, each brushed with melted butter. Let filo overlap sides of the pan. Sprinkle with half cup of nut mixture. Repeat, alternating 2 sheets of buttered filo and nut mixture, ending with filo.
6. After placing the final sheet of filo, fold the hanging filo onto the top sheet to create a neat seal. Using a razor blade or very sharp knife, cut through the top layer of filo into lengthwise strips about 1 1/2 inches wide, then diagonally into diamonds.
7. Bake at 325 degrees F. for 1 hour or until golden brown. Remove from oven and place on rack to cool slightly. Pour cooled honey syrup over the baklava. Stop pouring when there is no more syrup or when the baklava stops sizzling. Cool and serve. Makes about 4 dozen pieces.
